title: Dividing a 4-Digit Number by a 2-Digit Number

steps.explanation: We will divide digit by digit from left to right, bringing down digits as needed.

steps.instruction: How many times does 17 go into 28?
steps.work: 17 × 1 = 17, 17 × 2 = 34 (too big). So 17 goes into 28 one time. 28 - 17 = 11.
steps.explanation: We try multiples of 17 until we find the largest one that fits into 28.

steps.instruction: Bring down 5 to make 115. Divide 115 by 17.
steps.explanation: Bring down the next digit and repeat the process.

steps.instruction: Bring down 6 to make 136. Divide 136 by 17.

steps.explanation: Always verify by multiplying quotient × divisor to confirm.

title: Division with a Zero in the Quotient

steps.explanation: Sometimes a digit in the quotient is 0. Watch for this carefully.

steps.instruction: How many times does 21 go into 63?
Bring down 8. How many times does 21 go into 8?
steps.work: 21 does not go into 8 at all. Write 0 in the quotient. Bring down 4 to get 84.
steps.explanation: When the divisor is larger than the current number, the quotient digit is 0. Don't skip it!
The zero in the middle of 304 is essential. A common error is writing 34 instead of 304.
